scenarios.name: Conference / Event Follow-Up
scenarios.description: Outreach to contacts made at in-person or virtual industry events. Prioritizes recency of the encounter — Email 1 goes within 48 hours while the interaction is still fresh.
scenarios.targetAudience: Community bank and credit union executives met at industry events

scenarios.name: Cold — DMI Metrics Education Series
scenarios.description: Symptom-awareness education sequence for cold, unmet contacts. Opens by naming Prismm, what it does, and the beneficiary-tracking gap directly, no withheld hook. Each subsequent email teaches one real Deposit Mortality Index metric (Growth Coverage Ratio, ten-year dollar runoff) and offers to run that specific analysis for the recipient's institution. First of two sequences in campaign CAMP-DMI-01; hands off to scenario-003 roughly 2-3 weeks after the last touch.
scenarios.targetAudience: Retail, deposit, and experience leadership at banks and credit unions, national, bank-agnostic
scenarios.assetSize: Any — DMI scoring works across asset sizes, from mid-size community institutions to large national banks
scenarios.trigger: Cold outreach, no prior contact or event encounter

scenarios.name: Cold — DMI Peer Benchmark (Follow-On)
scenarios.description: Benchmark/direct-ask sequence, second half of the DMI campaign (CAMP-DMI-01). Sent to the same contact list roughly 2-3 weeks after scenario-002's education sequence completes, standing on its own rather than reading as continued fatigue. Leads with the composite DMI score, succession capture deficit, and peer percentile ranking, and closes with the most direct, concrete ask in the campaign.
scenarios.targetAudience: Retail, deposit, and experience leadership at banks and credit unions, national, bank-agnostic
scenarios.assetSize: Any — DMI scoring works across asset sizes, from mid-size community institutions to large national banks
scenarios.trigger: Follow-on to scenario-002, same contact list, 2-3 weeks after the education sequence ends
